entries.title: League Attempts To Reframe Bakugo As Recruitable

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The League tries to interpret Bakugo’s aggression and public image as evidence he can be recruited, but they misunderstand his core drive.
content.injection: At the League hideout, make the villains misread Bakugo’s violent temperament as villain potential while Bakugo remains fundamentally committed to winning as a hero.
constraints: Bakugo can be rude and defiant without being ideologically tempted by villainy.
antiLore: Do not make Bakugo genuinely consider joining the League in this arc.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Bakugo Refuses Villain Recruitment Without Softening

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Bakugo rejects the League because his ambition is to win as a hero, not because he suddenly becomes polite or emotionally open.
content.injection: When Bakugo faces the League, keep him abrasive, furious, and untempted; his morality is expressed through heroic ambition rather than gentleness.
constraints: His refusal must not erase his aggressive personality.
antiLore: Do not turn Bakugo into a villain sympathizer or a suddenly mild captive.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Shigaraki Is Still Dependent On All For One

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: During Kamino, Shigaraki is still a developing villain leader whose escape and survival depend heavily on All For One.
content.injection: In the Hideout Raid, portray Shigaraki as dangerous but not yet the independent final-stage threat; All For One remains the dominant mastermind.
constraints: Shigaraki can show growth and agency without surpassing All For One here.
antiLore: Do not import later awakened Shigaraki power or independence into Kamino.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: League Core Escapes While All For One Takes The Stage

context.label: All For One enters the battlefield

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: All For One intervenes to protect and evacuate Shigaraki’s League while drawing All Might into the decisive public battle.
content.injection: When All For One appears, shift the scene scale: the League’s survival becomes tied to his intervention, and All Might is forced into a public endgame fight.
constraints: All For One’s priority includes Shigaraki’s escape, not merely defeating All Might.
antiLore: Do not have the League casually overpower the pro raid on their own.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Dabi Identity Must Stay Unrevealed At Kamino

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Dabi is present as a League member, but his later identity reveal must not be known or stated during Kamino.
content.injection: During the Training Camp and Kamino arcs, write Dabi as a dangerous League villain without revealing his later family identity.
constraints: Suspicion or visual resemblance must not become confirmed knowledge.
antiLore: Do not call Dabi Toya or reveal his relationship to Endeavor before the proper war arc.

entries.title: Toga Is Not Yet A Fully Public Infiltration Threat

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Toga’s blood-taking has future consequences, but the heroes do not yet have full operational awareness of her later infiltration threat.
content.injection: Keep Toga’s future disguise danger latent in this arc; do not let heroes preemptively know all later uses of her Transform Quirk.
constraints: Her personality can be unsettling without full future plot leakage.
antiLore: Do not reveal or deploy later Toga infiltration outcomes early.

entries.title: Twice Spinner Magne And Compress Give The League Texture

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The League roster contains distinct personalities and motives; they are not interchangeable henchmen.
content.injection: When the League is present around Kamino, give members distinct voices and roles: Twice unstable, Spinner ideological, Magne forceful, Compress theatrical.
constraints: Their deeper later arcs must not be fully imported here.
antiLore: Do not write every League member as a generic silent minion.

entries.title: Kurogiri Enables League Mobility But Not Omniscience

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Kurogiri’s Warp Gate enables League movement and escape, but it does not make the League omniscient or immune to tactical surprise.
content.injection: Use Kurogiri’s warping as major mobility infrastructure for the League without making it a perfect answer to every tactical problem.
constraints: Warping depends on location knowledge, coordination, and Kurogiri’s condition.
antiLore: Do not make Kurogiri automatically know all hidden enemy positions.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

ability.name: Warp Gate
constraints: Provides portals but requires coordination and cannot replace intelligence.
antiLore: Do not treat Warp Gate as omniscient teleportation.

entries.title: Ragdoll Search Loss Matters Beyond The Camp

context.label: Nomu factory revealed as second raid site

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Ragdoll’s Search Quirk is taken by All For One, making the camp attack’s consequences extend beyond immediate injuries and capture.
content.injection: After Kamino reveals Ragdoll’s condition, treat Search as lost from the Pussycats and dangerous in enemy hands, while avoiding later Shigaraki-specific uses before their arc.
constraints: The loss is personal, tactical, and institutional.
antiLore: Do not let Ragdoll continue using Search after it is stolen.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

ability.name: Search
antiLore: Do not give Ragdoll her Search functionality post-theft.

entries.title: Bakugo Captivity Shows His Pride Under Pressure

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis
All For One exposed at Kamino

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Bakugo’s captivity tests his pride under real villain pressure, but his refusal is direct and uncompromising.
content.injection: In captivity, write Bakugo as angry and defiant under pressure; he is vulnerable by situation, not psychologically converted.
constraints: He can be afraid or cornered without conceding.
antiLore: Do not make Bakugo calmly negotiate villain membership.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: All For One Treats Shigaraki As Successor Project

context.label: All For One enters the battlefield

coordinates.label: U.A. before dorm system under public crisis

content.fact: All For One’s actions at Kamino prioritize Shigaraki’s survival and future as much as immediate victory.
content.injection: At Kamino, write All For One as protecting a long-term successor project; Shigaraki is not expendable to him.
